title: Somatic cell reprogramming using NF-κB inhibitory strategies
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bf6yi","text":"This protocol describes somatic cell reprogramming methodologies using NF-κB inhibitors step-by-step. Thus, we have used NF-κB pharmacological and genetic inhibitory strategies for the reprogramming of human fibroblasts from normal and accelerated ageing. These strategies can be used for implementing reprogramming methodologies in ageing conditions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Retroviral vectors expressing human OCT4, SOX2, KLF4 and c-MYC were transfected in HEK-293T cells (5x10 6 cells per 10 cm plate each) along with packaging plasmids to produce retrovirus.
Human primary fibroblasts were seeded at a density of 1x10 5 cells per well in 6-well plates.
Twenty four h after plating, cells were infected with retroviral supernatants and the same procedure was repeated every 12 h for 2 days (a total of four additions) (Figure 1).

The day after last infection (day 3), medium was replaced with fibroblasts medium, and kept for three more days. NF-κB inhibitors were added at this point and maintained along the reprogramming process (IKK-VII, 0.5 µM and sodium salicylate, 0.2 mM) (Note 1).
At day 7, cells were trypsinized and reseeded on feeder plates (Note 2).
On day 8, medium was changed to human iPSC medium.
Cultures were maintained with daily medium changes for 4 weeks. At that point, colonies were isolated and expanded on a feeder layer or Tra-1-60 stained for evaluating reprogramming efficiency.

Retroviral vectors expressing human OCT4, SOX2, KLF4, c-MYC, IκBα-SR or IKK2-KI were transfected in HEK-293T cells (5x10 6 cells per 10 cm plate each) along with packaging plasmids to produce retrovirus.
Human primary fibroblasts were seeded at a density of 1x10 5 cells per well in 6-well plates.
Twenty four h after plating, cells were infected with retroviral supernatants and the same procedure was repeated every 12 h for 2 days (a total of four additions).
Cells were trypsinized at day 7 and reseeded on feeder plates. On day 8, medium was changed to human iPSC medium and cultures were maintained with daily medium changes for 4 weeks.

Notes 1) We have observed that NF-κB-inhibition during the first two weeks is sufficient to achieve the complete effect on reprogramming efficiency. Thus, NF-κB inhibitors could be removed from iPSC medium with no effect on reprogramming efficiency after that time point. 2) Human iPSCs were cultured on mitomycin-inactivated mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs).

NF-κB activation impairs somatic cell reprogramming in ageing by Clara Soria-Valles, Fernando G. Osorio, Ana Gutiérrez-Fernández, Alejandro De Los Angeles, Clara Bueno, Pablo Menéndez, José I. Martín-Subero, George Q. Daley, José M. P. Freije, and Carlos López-Otín published in Nature Cell Biology on 12 Oct, 2015 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/ncb3207
Figure 1. Scheme of somatic cell reprogramming process with NF-kB inhibitors
